coverage_map.lo: Explain what 'testing a specification' means — comparing actual AI output to what you specified.
Apply the three-question verification check to any AI output to decide whether it passes.
Classify a test result as full pass, partial pass, or full fail, and describe what each looks like.
Record a test result using a simple pass/fail table mapped to each part of the specification.
Identify three common failure patterns — format drift, constraint overflow, and task drift — and explain what causes each.
Work through a complete test on a real specification and its AI output across two domains.

All concepts tested (verification, three-question check, pass/fail classification, the table, three failure patterns) are explicitly defined in the corpus. Q5 scenario mirrors the health-domain worked example from corpus §5.6. No forward references or unexplained terms detected.

verdict: Pass — all five checks clean. Positional bias at max_share=0.40 (boundary, not over). Length bias 0/5 longest-correct. Distractor plausibility absurd_rate=0.0. All 6 LOs covered 1-for-1. No scope creep or jargon.
